headline: These Dividend Kings Have Raised Their Dividends for 50+ Years
summary: If your plan is to earn passive income from stocks, you’d might as well pick out the ones that offer dividend growth. When a company has raised its dividend distributions for a very long time, that’s a reassuring sign for investors. There are legions of stocks out there to choose from, but most of them ... These Dividend Kings Have Raised Their Dividends for 50+ Years

headline: Dow Jones set to open lower but Broadcom poised for gains
summary: US futures were pointing to a softer open on Wall Street on Thursday, after Iran de-escalation hopes earlier boosted European markets before being played down. Dow Jones futures were down 0.5%, with the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq both slipping 0.2%. A day earlier, US stocks closed solidly...

headline: U.S. Treasury Yields Rise As Inflation Worries Continue
summary: Treasury yields rose as concerns about the inflationary impact of the U.S.-Iran conflict caused investors to price in higher borrowing costs.

raw.headline: U.S. Treasury Yields Rise As Inflation Worries Continue
raw.summary: Treasury yields rose as concerns about the inflationary impact of the U.S.-Iran conflict caused investors to price in higher borrowing costs.

headline: U.S. Economic Data Boosts Global Market Sentiment, Middle East Conflict Casts Shadow
summary: Markets showed resilience even as the U.S. Senate green-lit the U.S. attacks against Iran amid an escalating Middle East conflict.

raw.headline: U.S. Economic Data Boosts Global Market Sentiment, Middle East Conflict Casts Shadow

headline: Dollar Rises as Strong Data Dampen Rate
summary: The dollar rose after stronger-than-expected U.S. economic data further reduced the prospect of further interest-rate cuts by the Federal Reserve. The ISM services purchasing mangers' index jumped to 56.

raw.headline: Dollar Rises as Strong Data Dampen Rate
raw.summary: The dollar rose after stronger-than-expected U.S. economic data further reduced the prospect of further interest-rate cuts by the Federal Reserve. The ISM services purchasing mangers' index jumped to 56.

headline: 1 of Wall Street’s Favorite Stock to Target This Week and 2 We Question
summary: The stocks in this article have caught Wall Street’s attention in a big way, with price targets implying returns above 20%. But investors should take these forecasts with a grain of salt because analysts typically say nice things about companies so their firms can win business in other product lines like M&A advisory.
